reasoning: This is a direct invocation of the fully verified lemma `tla_forall_a_p_leads_to_q_a_is_stable` which contains no external_body axioms. The proof is entirely SMT-checked; stability of tla_forall from component stability is a correct temporal logic result.

`stable(X).satisfied_by(ex)` unfolds to `X.satisfied_by(ex) ==> always(X).satisfied_by(ex)`. Given `X.satisfied_by(ex)` as a precondition, modus ponens yields `always(X).satisfied_by(ex)`. This is correct logic with no external_body dependency.

`stable(X) = X ==> always(X)`, so when `!X.satisfied_by(ex)`, the implication is vacuously true. This is standard propositional logic, not a spec gap — `valid(stable(X))` is not expected to provide information when X doesn't hold.
